what: Added Python Gmail→Sheets ingestion for GAM CSV email attachments with OAuth, env config, label deduplication, and documentation.
why: Automate daily extraction into a sheet consumed by upstream reporting.
implications: Operators must supply OAuth credentials, spreadsheet ID, and a tuned Gmail query; CSV column layout must stay stable across runs.

what: Documented PMC Mula Report CSV columns and attachment regex; added samples/pmc_mula_report.sample.csv; gitignored local PMC _ Mula Report.csv downloads.
why: Align config with the actual Penske export filename and schema.
implications: Use GAM_ATTACHMENT_REGEX for PMC Mula attachment selection; keep production CSVs out of version control.

what: Default Gmail query and docs now target example subject `Mula Inc Mail - Report_ PMC _ Mula Report …` using stable `subject:"PMC _ Mula Report"`; `.env.example` enables attachment regex by default.
why: Weekly date span in subject must not be part of the search string.
implications: Ingestion matches recurring Mula report mail without per-week query updates; add `from:` if subject collisions appear.

what: Recorded Penske KVP GAM destination spreadsheet ID `1TDZGpXNrQ7qoH9oePzx0KKSrBKY3iHYXIcG_5AN9VRE` and canonical URL in `.env.example` and README.
why: Operators need a single configured target sheet for upstream reporting.
implications: OAuth principal must have edit access to that file; `GAM_SHEET_TAB` must match an existing tab name.

what: Default and documented target tab name set to `Data` (Penske KVP GAM worksheet tab).
why: Actual workbook tab is named Data, not GAM_Data.
implications: `.env.example`, code default `GAM_SHEET_TAB`, and README now use Data.

what: Recorded Google Apps Script project ID `1h7Ad1fzEw2BlNRgbU6s3YlzIlkxWiRkQyf-aEoxOLLZHYn7UwtaA2nv5` in README and techContext.
why: Operators need a stable reference to the linked Apps Script project.
implications: Python ingest does not require this ID unless we add Apps Script API integration.

what: Added `apps-script/` clasp project: `.clasp.json`, `appsscript.json`, `src/` rootDir, `package.json` with `@google/clasp`, `.claspignore`, npm lockfile; README clasp workflow; `.gitignore` node_modules.
why: Enable version-controlled push/pull against the existing Apps Script project.
implications: Developers run `cd apps-script && npm install && clasp login && npm run pull`; credentials stay in ~/.clasprc.json.

what: Penske KVP GAM spreadsheet shared with the Google account used for OAuth/ingestion.
why: Sheets API append requires edit access for the authorized identity.
implications: Sheets writes should succeed once token.json is created for that user; Gmail query must target mail visible to the same account if applicable.

what: Added Apps Script daily trigger helpers in `apps-script/src/Code.gs` (`installDailyTrigger`, `scheduledRun` stub) and README subsection.
why: Operator asked for Apps Script time-driven trigger setup alongside clasp.
implications: Merge with `clasp pull` output before push if cloud project already has code; Python ingest remains primary for Gmail→Sheet.

what: Moved `appsscript.json` to `apps-script/src/` so clasp `rootDir` uploads include manifest; `clasp push --force` used when default push skips.
why: clasp requires manifest under content directory matching rootDir.
implications: README notes manifest path and `--force`; operators need full FS access for ~/.clasprc.json when pushing from sandboxed CI.

what: Added repo-root `package.json` with `clasp:*` npm scripts delegating to `apps-script/` via `--prefix`.
why: Running `npm run push:force` from repo root failed with ENOENT (no root package.json).
implications: Use `npm run clasp:push:force` from root or `cd apps-script` for existing script names.

what: Apps Script daily trigger installed; `scheduledRun` execution log shows tab Data and lastRow 0 (empty sheet).
why: Operator completed clasp push and trigger setup.
implications: Python Gmail→Sheet ingest or manual data will increase lastRow; optional to extend `scheduledRun` for alerts when lastRow stays 0.

what: Added full Gmail→CSV→Sheet ingest in `apps-script/src/Ingest.gs` with Script properties (`GAM_*`) and `scheduledRun` calling `ingestGmailReportToSheet`; `setupDefaultScriptProperties` helper.
why: Operator wanted end-to-end flow in Apps Script like other projects.
implications: Re-push with clasp; re-auth for Gmail; Python path remains for CI/local; CSV parsing uses Utilities.parseCsv (comma-oriented).

what: Default `GAM_GMAIL_QUERY` switched from `subject:"PMC _ Mula Report"` to quoted phrase `"PMC _ Mula Report"` plus has:attachment/newer_than/in:anywhere; aligns Python `.env.example` and config default.
why: Gmail UI phrase search worked while subject-prefixed query returned zero threads in Apps Script.
implications: Update Script properties or rerun `setupDefaultScriptProperties`; users with existing .env may adjust query.

what: Apps Script ingest: use `thread.addLabel(label)` instead of `GmailMessage.addLabel` (was TypeError at runtime).
why: Message-level addLabel not exposed reliably in Apps Script Gmail API surface.
implications: Re-clasp push; labels entire thread after append (same dedupe intent).

what: Apps Script `appendRows_` now builds a padded rectangular matrix (max column width across rows) before `setValues`.
why: setValues threw row/column mismatch when CSV rows had uneven column counts vs range sized from first row only.
implications: Re-clasp push; logs appendRows_ dimensions for debugging.

what: Apps Script `appendRows_` uses `getRange(startRow, 1, numRows, numCols)` — Sheet API 4-arg form is (startRow, startCol, **numRows**, **numColumns**), not last-row index.
why: Passing endRow (63) as third arg reserved 63 rows while data had 31 rows.
implications: Re-clasp push; ingest append should succeed.

what: Apps Script ingest completed successfully: two Mula report threads, 31 and 17 data rows appended to sheet tab Data.
why: Operator confirmed execution log after getRange fix.
implications: Daily trigger will ingest future reports; labeled threads skipped on rerun.

what: Initialized git, excluded local PDF from repo (`*.pdf` in gitignore), pushed `main` to github.com/lorenzlk/penske-gam-ingestion.
why: Publish project source for Penske GAM ingestion.
implications: Clone/pull use HTTPS remote; secrets remain untracked.

what: Sheet dedupe by `gmail_message_id` column A: `sheets_ops.get_existing_gmail_message_ids`, Python `run.py` skip+label; Apps Script `loadExistingMessageIds_` + skip+label.
why: Prevent duplicate rows if labels cleared or job races; user clearing sheet requested safety.
implications: Re-clasp push; pull column A once per run (same-thread multi-append still unique message id).
